evals.prompt: Set up this TypeScript monorepo so coding agents stop reading every package for each task. Create a small AGENTS.md, a context router, and only verified on-demand docs. Do not change application code.
evals.expected_output: A grounded bootstrap that inventories the repository, derives behavior-oriented routes, creates a compact canonical entrypoint and linked docs/agent router, adds realistic routing cases, validates the result, and reports uncertainty without claiming unmeasured savings.
assertions: No product source file is modified.
The root entrypoint links to docs/agent/index.md and contains a progressive reading protocol.
Routes identify first-read documents, code roots, tests, overlays, and fallback behavior.
Commands and architecture claims are supported by repository evidence.
Validation and an estimated context budget are reported.

evals.prompt: Our AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are huge and contradict each other. Refactor them into one canonical guide plus task-specific context, but preserve every valid safety and release constraint.
evals.expected_output: A preservation-first refactor that classifies existing rules, resolves or reports conflicts, keeps one canonical source, creates only thin client-specific deltas, routes domain details out of always-loaded files, and documents all moved or uncertain constraints.
assertions: Existing human constraints are not silently deleted.
One canonical source owns universal facts and duplicate prose is reduced.
Client-specific files contain only pointers and genuine client deltas.
Conflicts and their resolution are explicitly reported.
The result does not preload all routed documentation.

evals.prompt: Audit our current agent-context setup without editing anything. Find broken routes, stale paths, duplicated commands, unreachable docs, and places where every task is paying for domain-specific context.
evals.expected_output: A read-only audit with mechanical validation, context-budget estimates, evidence-backed findings ranked by severity, and a surgical remediation plan. It must not write repository files or present guesses as facts.
assertions: The audit mode performs no repository writes.
Findings include evidence paths and distinguish errors from warnings.
Broken links, stale paths, duplication, reachability, and always-loaded size are examined.
Token counts are labeled as estimates.
The remediation plan is incremental rather than a blind regeneration.

evals.prompt: We added a payments service and moved authorization into a shared package. Refresh the existing context router, keep our editorial notes, and update only what drifted.
evals.expected_output: A surgical refresh that inventories changed boundaries, updates route ownership and overlays, preserves valid human notes, revises routing cases, removes stale references, and validates the final graph without rewriting unrelated documents.
assertions: Existing documentation is updated surgically rather than regenerated wholesale.
Responsibility drift is handled, not merely path replacement.
Valid human-authored notes are preserved.
Routing cases cover the new service and shared authorization overlay.
Validation reports no broken required links.
